name: REST resource API
description: Resource-oriented API with predictable HTTP verbs and status codes
when_to_use: CRUD-heavy applications public APIs admin panels and integrations
trade_offs: Can become chatty for complex graph reads
implementation_notes: Use versioned routes input DTOs cursor pagination and consistent error envelopes
keywords: rest http crud endpoint pagination openapi

name: GraphQL read model
description: Client-shaped query API for complex nested reads
when_to_use: Dashboards mobile apps and clients needing flexible field selection
trade_offs: Requires query complexity limits and resolver discipline
implementation_notes: Use DataLoader or batched repositories to prevent N+1 and enforce depth limits
keywords: graphql nested query dashboard resolver dataloader

name: gRPC service API
description: Schema-first RPC over HTTP/2 with protobuf contracts
when_to_use: Internal service-to-service calls low latency polyglot systems
trade_offs: Harder for browsers and human debugging than REST
implementation_notes: Use protobuf versioning deadlines retries and health checks
keywords: grpc protobuf rpc internal low latency

name: tRPC type-safe API
description: End-to-end TypeScript API with inferred client types
when_to_use: Full-stack TypeScript apps where server and client share repo ownership
trade_offs: Tightly couples client and server TypeScript boundaries
implementation_notes: Keep procedures thin and delegate business rules to services

name: Cursor pagination
description: Stable pagination based on ordered cursor tokens
when_to_use: Large or frequently changing lists feeds audit logs and activity streams
trade_offs: More complex than offset for arbitrary page jumps
implementation_notes: Order by immutable or monotonic fields and include deterministic tie breaker
keywords: cursor pagination infinite scroll feed large list

name: Offset pagination
description: Limit and offset pagination for simple stable lists
when_to_use: Small admin tables low-volume reports and static datasets
trade_offs: Can skip or duplicate rows under concurrent writes
implementation_notes: Always enforce max limit and indexes on order/filter fields

name: Compatible additive versioning
description: Evolve API by adding fields and preserving existing behavior
when_to_use: Public APIs and long-lived client integrations
trade_offs: Requires contract tests and deprecation discipline
implementation_notes: Add fields only avoid changing semantics and publish migration windows

name: Problem Details error envelope
description: Standard structured errors for clients and logs
when_to_use: Any HTTP API that returns validation auth or domain errors
trade_offs: Requires consistent mapping across handlers
implementation_notes: Use type title status detail instance and correlation id fields

name: OpenAPI contract first
description: Design and validate API contracts before implementation
when_to_use: Public APIs partner integrations and generated clients
trade_offs: Contract changes require review discipline
implementation_notes: Generate server stubs clients and contract tests from OpenAPI
keywords: openapi contract first schema client sdk

name: JSON API style resources
description: Standardize relationships filtering and sparse fieldsets
when_to_use: CRUD APIs with rich relationships and multiple clients
trade_offs: Can feel verbose for simple APIs
implementation_notes: Use consistent resource types links includes and error objects
keywords: json api resource relationship include sparse fields

name: BFF API
description: Backend-for-frontend tailored to one client experience
when_to_use: Mobile web and admin surfaces with different data needs
trade_offs: Duplicates logic if domain services are not shared
implementation_notes: Keep BFF thin and call shared application services
keywords: bff backend for frontend mobile dashboard

name: Server-Sent Events
description: One-way server to client event stream
when_to_use: Notifications progress updates dashboards and simple real-time feeds
trade_offs: Not suitable for bidirectional chat or binary streams
implementation_notes: Use retry ids heartbeat and auth-aware connection limits
keywords: sse server sent events progress realtime

name: WebSocket gateway
description: Bidirectional long-lived connection for real-time interaction
when_to_use: Chat multiplayer collaborative editing and live trading
trade_offs: Needs connection lifecycle scaling and backpressure
implementation_notes: Authenticate connection and route messages through services

name: Pre-signed upload URL
description: Upload large files directly to object storage
when_to_use: Media documents imports and user-generated files
trade_offs: A callback or finalize step is needed for consistency
implementation_notes: Issue short-lived signed URL then verify object metadata before commit
keywords: file upload presigned url s3 object storage

name: Consumer-driven contract tests
description: Verify providers do not break client expectations
when_to_use: Microservices and independently deployed clients
trade_offs: Requires maintaining useful consumer contracts
implementation_notes: Run contract tests in CI before deploy and version breaking changes

name: Explicit filtering DSL
description: Constrain supported filters sorts and includes
when_to_use: List endpoints with user filters search and reporting
trade_offs: Too much flexibility can create slow queries
implementation_notes: Whitelist fields operators and index-supported combinations
keywords: filter sort include query api whitelist

name: JSON Merge Patch
description: Partial update format for resource fields
when_to_use: Simple PATCH endpoints with nullable field semantics
trade_offs: Ambiguous for array operations and explicit null handling
implementation_notes: Use content type application merge patch and validate patched resource

name: JSON Patch
description: Operation based partial update for precise changes
when_to_use: Complex document edits arrays and collaborative resource changes
trade_offs: More complex to validate and authorize per operation
implementation_notes: Validate every operation path and apply against current version
keywords: json patch operation replace add remove

name: Response compression
description: Compress text responses to reduce transfer size
when_to_use: Large JSON HTML GraphQL and public API responses
trade_offs: CPU cost and compression side channels need care
implementation_notes: Use gzip or brotli for text and avoid compressing secrets with attacker input

name: ETag conditional requests
description: Avoid sending unchanged resources
when_to_use: Read-heavy resources docs profiles catalog and config
trade_offs: Requires stable validators and cache semantics
implementation_notes: Return ETag and support If-None-Match with 304 responses

name: Request size limits
description: Reject oversized payloads before expensive parsing
when_to_use: File metadata JSON APIs webhook receivers and public endpoints
trade_offs: Too small limits can block valid clients
implementation_notes: Set per-route body limits and stream large uploads elsewhere

name: API rate-limit headers
description: Expose quota state to well-behaved clients
when_to_use: Public APIs partner APIs and tenant quota endpoints
trade_offs: May reveal some capacity information
implementation_notes: Return RateLimit-Limit RateLimit-Remaining and RateLimit-Reset

name: Idempotent PUT semantics
description: Make full replacement writes safe to repeat
when_to_use: Resource replacement endpoints and infrastructure style APIs
trade_offs: Harder when writes trigger side effects
implementation_notes: Ensure same request can be replayed without duplicate effects

name: Batch endpoint with item results
description: Process many small operations in one request
when_to_use: Bulk admin actions imports and sync APIs
trade_offs: Can hide partial failures and create large transactions
implementation_notes: Return per-item status and cap batch size
keywords: batch api bulk operation item result

name: Webhook subscription API
description: Allow clients to register callback endpoints
when_to_use: Platforms integrations and event notification systems
trade_offs: Requires delivery security retry and lifecycle management
implementation_notes: Verify ownership sign payloads and expose retry status

name: Webhook delivery log API
description: Expose delivery attempts and failures to integrators
when_to_use: Developer platforms billing and automation products
trade_offs: Requires storing delivery metadata safely
implementation_notes: Store status attempts response code and redacted response excerpt

name: Machine token API
description: Issue scoped credentials for service accounts
when_to_use: Automation CI integrations and server-to-server calls
trade_offs: Token lifecycle needs rotation and audit
implementation_notes: Use scoped tokens expiry rotation and last used metadata

name: OAuth client credentials flow
description: Authenticate machine-to-machine integrations
when_to_use: Partner APIs internal services and automation
trade_offs: No user context unless explicitly modeled
implementation_notes: Use least privilege scopes and client secret rotation

name: Deprecation headers
description: Warn clients about API sunset and migrations
when_to_use: Public APIs with planned endpoint changes
trade_offs: Requires policy and client communication
implementation_notes: Return Deprecation Sunset and Link headers with migration docs

name: API changelog contract
description: Publish behavioral and schema changes
when_to_use: External APIs SDKs and partner integrations
trade_offs: Needs release discipline
implementation_notes: Document additions deprecations breaking changes and migration windows

name: GraphQL persisted queries
description: Allow only registered query hashes in production
when_to_use: Public GraphQL APIs mobile apps and high-traffic clients
trade_offs: Adds build or registration workflow
implementation_notes: Store approved query hashes and reject unknown expensive queries

name: GraphQL query complexity limit
description: Prevent expensive nested queries from exhausting resources
when_to_use: GraphQL endpoints with flexible clients
trade_offs: Complexity scoring must match resolver cost
implementation_notes: Set depth node and cost budgets per role or client

name: Deadline propagation
description: Propagate request deadlines through gRPC calls
when_to_use: Internal RPC chains and latency-sensitive services
trade_offs: Requires every caller and callee to respect context
implementation_notes: Reject work after deadline and include deadline in downstream calls

name: Protobuf field reservation
description: Prevent accidental reuse of removed fields
when_to_use: Long-lived protobuf contracts and multi-version clients
trade_offs: Requires schema discipline
implementation_notes: Reserve removed field numbers and names in proto files

name: Connection heartbeat
description: Detect dead real-time clients and intermediaries
when_to_use: WebSocket SSE and long polling systems
trade_offs: Heartbeat traffic adds overhead
implementation_notes: Send ping or heartbeat and close stale connections

name: Backpressure-aware streaming
description: Slow down or disconnect clients that cannot keep up
when_to_use: Real-time feeds logs and event streams
trade_offs: Can drop slow clients under load
implementation_notes: Bound buffers and define drop replay or disconnect policy

name: SDK generation boundary
description: Generate client SDKs from stable contracts
when_to_use: Public APIs with many consumers
trade_offs: Generated SDKs can expose poor API design
implementation_notes: Generate from OpenAPI or protobuf after contract review

name: Golden API examples
description: Keep canonical request and response examples in tests
when_to_use: Public APIs docs and regression prevention
trade_offs: Examples can become stale without CI
implementation_notes: Validate examples against schema and docs

name: Localized error messages
description: Return stable error codes with optional localized messages
when_to_use: Consumer apps and international products
trade_offs: Server localization can complicate clients
implementation_notes: Expose stable machine code and keep localization separate

name: Data minimization response
description: Return only fields needed by the client
when_to_use: PII-heavy APIs and public endpoints
trade_offs: May require multiple DTOs per use case
implementation_notes: Use explicit response DTOs and avoid returning persistence entities
